name: Garganta de las Nogaleas Trail
name_original: Chorrera de las Nogaleras – running circular desde Navaconcejo
description: Jog the 3.1-mile Garganta de las Nogaleas Trail, a moderate route with 925 feet of gain, known for its dramatic waterfalls and scenic views.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Navaconcejo are set within the Jerte Valley, a region characterized by its river gorges, extensive cherry orchards, and diverse forests of oak and chestnut trees. The topography includes the lower reaches of the Gredos Massif, offering trails that range from gentle riverside paths along the River Jerte to more challenging ascents. This area is known for its numerous waterfalls and natural pools, providing varied terrain for running. Best jogging routes around Navaconcejo The most popular jogging route is Garganta de las Nogaleas Trail, a 3.1 miles (5.0 km) trail that takes about 45 minutes to complete. This route showcases stunning waterfalls and panoramic views. Another top favourite among local runners is Cascada del Caozo Trail, a moderate 5.1 miles (8.3 km) path. This trail winds through diverse forest and river landscapes to the impressive Cascada del Caozo. Local runners also love the Route of the Pilones – running circular from Jerte through the Garganta de los Infiernos, a 6.3 miles (10.2 km) trail leading through the Garganta de los Infiernos Natural Reserve, often completed in about 1 hour 20 minutes. Jogging around Navaconcejo is defined by river valleys, cascading waterfalls, and lush forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from riverside runs to mountain ascents. The routes in Navaconcejo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 240 runners have used komoot to explore Navaconcejo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes can I find around Navaconcejo?
A: Navaconcejo offers a diverse network of over 25 running routes, catering to various fitness levels. These include paths ranging from moderate to difficult, ensuring there's a suitable trail for almost every runner.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging routes in Navaconcejo?
A: While many routes in Navaconcejo are rated moderate to difficult due to the varied topography, the region also offers gentler riverside paths along the &lt;strong&gt;River Jerte&lt;/strong&gt;. These provide a pleasant, flatter terrain suitable for easier jogs or beginners. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991200556/garganta-de-las-nogaleas-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garganta de las Nogaleas Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a popular, relatively easy 3.1-mile (5.0 km) circular route showcasing stunning waterfalls.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on the running trails?
A: The running trails around Navaconcejo are rich in natural beauty. You'll encounter spectacular gorges, numerous waterfalls, and distinctive natural pools, especially within the &lt;strong&gt;Garganta de los Infiernos Natural Reserve&lt;/strong&gt;. The region is also characterized by its vibrant river gorges, extensive cherry orchards, and diverse forests of oak and chestnut trees. Notable features include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4204503" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nogaleas Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt; and the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4360769" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caozo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular running routes available?
A: Yes, Navaconcejo features several excellent circular running routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991200556/garganta-de-las-nogaleas-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garganta de las Nogaleas Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 3.1-mile (5.0 km) loop. Another highly-rated circular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990912807/route-of-the-pilones-running-circular-from-jerte-through-the-garganta-de-los-infiernos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Pilones – running circular from Jerte through the Garganta de los Infiernos&lt;/a&gt;, a 6.3-mile (10.2 km) trail.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions near the running paths?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can discover several interesting landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3243264" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Pilones Natural Pools&lt;/a&gt; within the Garganta de los Infiernos are a unique sight. You might also encounter historical elements like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4267031" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carlos V Bridge&lt;/a&gt;. The region's cherry orchards are also a significant attraction, especially during the spring blossom season.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Navaconcejo?
A: The running routes in Navaconcejo are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.0 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 240 runners have explored the varied terrain, often praising the stunning waterfalls, lush forests, and the diverse options for different ability levels, from riverside runs to mountain ascents.

Q: When is the best time of year to go jogging in Navaconcejo?
A: The Jerte Valley is particularly stunning during the spring cherry blossom season, which transforms the landscape into a breathtaking spectacle, making it an ideal time for scenic runs. The area's abundant rainfall ensures a strong flow in its numerous gorges and waterfalls throughout much of the year, offering beautiful views regardless of the season. Summer offers opportunities for a refreshing dip in natural pools after a run.

Q: Are there any routes that offer panoramic viewpoints of the Jerte Valley?
A: Yes, several routes provide expansive vistas of the Jerte Valley. The &lt;strong&gt;Nogaleas Waterfalls – Panoramic view of the Jerte Valley loop from Rebollar&lt;/strong&gt; is a 6.6-mile (10.6 km) trail specifically designed to offer stunning panoramic views. You can also find viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5638307" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorrero de la Virgen Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; along various trails.

Q: Can I find routes that lead to impressive waterfalls?
A: Absolutely. Navaconcejo is renowned for its waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990926132/cascada-del-caozo-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada del Caozo Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 5.1-mile (8.2 km) path that leads directly to the impressive 35-meter &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4360769" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caozo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991200556/garganta-de-las-nogaleas-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garganta de las Nogaleas Trail&lt;/a&gt; also showcases multiple stunning waterfalls.

Q: Are there any challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: For experienced runners seeking a challenge, the topography around Navaconcejo includes the lower reaches of the &lt;strong&gt;Gredos Massif&lt;/strong&gt;, offering more demanding mountain ascents. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991207739/cueva-de-la-serrana-trail-run-from-garganta-la-olla-rt-01-rt-02" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de La Serrana – trail run from Garganta la Olla&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 10.7 km route with significant elevation gain, provide a rigorous workout. The historic &lt;strong&gt;Camino Real (PR-CC 15)&lt;/strong&gt; also offers a challenging run with ascents through cherry and chestnut groves.

Q: Are there opportunities for a refreshing dip after a run?
A: Yes, Navaconcejo is perfect for a post-run cool-down. The &lt;strong&gt;River Jerte&lt;/strong&gt;, described as a 'crystalline river,' flows through the town and offers several natural bathing areas directly within Navaconcejo, such as 'La Mora,' 'El Pilar,' and 'El Cristo.' Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3243264" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Pilones Natural Pools&lt;/a&gt; in the Garganta de los Infiernos Natural Reserve are unique swimming holes ideal for cooling down.
tips: This is one of the most popular and recognized tours in the province of Cáceres. It is an itinerary with 6 waterfalls and several viewpoints with very little technical difficulty, ideal to do with the whole family, even with the little ones. Highly recommended to go in the spring or summer months; the wooded path will give you very good shade and the waters of the chorreras are very refreshing.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Garganta de las Nogaleas Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts in Navaconcejo, within the Jerte Valley. You can typically find parking near the trailhead in the town.

faqs.question: Is the Garganta de las Nogaleas Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally accessible for dogs, but they should be kept on a leash to protect the local wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Always practice Leave No Trace principles.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is beautiful year-round, but for impressive water flow at the waterfalls, winter and spring (following snowmelt and heavy rains) are ideal. Late March and early April also offer the spectacular sight of cherry blossoms in the surrounding Jerte Valley.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the trail or the Valle del Jerte?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the Garganta de las Nogaleas Trail or the Valle del Jerte.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or casual joggers?
faqs.answer: The trail is rated as moderate difficulty. While some sections are easy, it features an intense and continuous uphill climb with significant elevation gain over a short distance. Joggers should have good fitness and be prepared for varied terrain, including natural stairs and irregular surfaces. It's more suited for experienced trail runners seeking a challenge.

faqs.question: What natural features and views can I expect to see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route is famous for its dramatic waterfalls, earning it the nickname 'Route of the Waterfalls.' You'll pass by numerous cascades, including the prominent Nogaleas Waterfalls. The trail is immersed in a dense riverside forest with oaks, chestnuts, and ash trees. As you ascend, you'll also get panoramic views of Navaconcejo and the wider Jerte Valley.

faqs.question: Is the Garganta de las Nogaleas Trail a loop?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Garganta de las Nogaleas Trail is a circular route, meaning it's a loop that brings you back to your starting point.

faqs.question: Does the trail connect with other routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is primarily known as the Ruta Garganta de las Nogaleas. It also passes through sections of the Valle del Jerte, Avenida de la Virgen del Pilar, and another segment of Avenida de la Virgen del Pilar.

faqs.question: When is the best time to see the waterfalls at their fullest?
faqs.answer: For the most impressive water flow, plan your visit during winter and spring. This is when snowmelt and heavy rains contribute to the waterfalls being at their most spectacular.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the trail?
faqs.answer: While the komoot tour suggests a jogging duration of approximately 46 minutes, hikers typically complete the 5-6 kilometer route in 1.5 to 2.5 hours. Joggers should account for the moderate difficulty and varied terrain, which may extend the time needed.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied and challenging. It starts with a cemented section before transitioning to a dirt trail. You'll encounter natural stairs, stone and wooden steps, and iron walkways crossing over the water. The surface can be irregular, and fallen leaves can make it slippery, especially in winter.
